text: A 45 cm parambulical incision is made for the insertion of a blunt port, followed by insufflation of the peritoneum. Under direct vision, two 5 mm ports are placed in the left lower quadrant and suprapubic midline. A disc of skin and subcutaneous fat is excised from the pre-marked stoma site in the right lower quadrant.

The patient is positioned in a moderately steep Trendelenburg with the left side elevated. The surgeon opens the left lateral peritoneal reflection alongside the distal sigmoid colon to identify and protect the left ureter. The sigmoid and descending colon are mobilized medially in a cephalad direction by dissecting along the white line of Toldt.
The surgeon carefully avoids mobilizing the kidney by maintaining dissection within the medial aspect of the paracolic gutter.

The surgeon mobilizes the splenic flexure by performing medial dissection of the omentum from the distal transverse colon and lateral dissection of the colon from the retroperitoneum. The final stages of this mobilization reveal the body and tail of the pancreas and the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV).

The patient is positioned in the Trendelenburg with the right side elevated. The surgeon moves the small bowel out of the pelvis, identifies and protects the right ureter. The cecum is retracted in a cephalad and medial direction, and the peritoneum around the base of the cecum and terminal ileum is scored, allowing entry into the retroperitoneal plane.

The surgeon develops the surgical plane medially to the inferior vena cava (IVC) and duodenum. The right lateral peritoneal reflection is opened, and the ascending colon is mobilized medially towards the duodenum. The hepatic flexure is mobilized until it is positioned below the level of the umbilicus, allowing the entire right colon to be moved to the midline, with the IVC and duodenum exposed and protected.
The surgeon completes the medial mobilization of the hepatic flexure, detaching it from retroperitoneal structures and exposing the duodenum, with the gyrotis fascia visible medially.

The surgeon shifts focus to the pelvis, continuing dissection distally along the left parorectal peritoneum. The precircular space is entered at the sacral promontory, and dissection proceeds distally within this space.

The surgeon scores the right parorectal peritoneum after identifying and protecting the right ureter. The dissection is joined in the pre-sacral space with the previous dissection on the left side. Using careful retraction and exposure, a full circumferential dissection of the rectum is performed in the extramasorectal bloodless plane down to the pelvic floor.

A sponge stick is placed in the vagina to identify the rectovaginal septum, aiding in the dissection of the rectum down to the top of the anal canal. The rectum is then transected at the pelvic floor using two firings of a laparoscopic linear articulated 45mm stapler.

The pneumoperitoneum is evacuated, and the entire colon is exteriorized from the terminal ileum to the transected edge of the rectum through the parenbiloculum incision. The mesentery is divided and ligated with double ligatures on the larger vessels, while preserving the ileocolic pedicle and the omentum.

The surgeon transects the terminal ileum with a linear stapler approximately four centimeters from the ileocecal valve. The specimen is removed, and the entire small bowel is exteriorized and examined for signs of Crohn's disease. The terminal ileum is retained for pouch creation, while the rest of the small bowel is returned to the abdominal cavity, ensuring correct orientation.

A 15-centimeter J-pouch is constructed and returned to the abdominal cavity, ensuring correct orientation of the pouch mesentery. The anus is gently dilated, and an EEA stapler is inserted with the spike emerging adjacent to the staple line. The J-pouch is then positioned into the pelvis, and the anvil is docked into the stapler.

The surgeon re-approximates the stapler before firing and removing it. A large round Jackson-Pratt drain is placed through the suprapubic port site. A loop of distal ileum above the pouch is brought out to the premarked stoma site in the right lower quadrant, where the ileostomy is matured using interrupted sutures.
